sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 511 of 2024 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.4157/2024 2. For orders on office objection No.1 3. For orders on CMA No.4158/2024 4. For orders on CMA No.4159/2024 5. For hearing of main case 11.03.2026 Mr. Shams Mohiuddin Ansari, advocate for the applicant Mr. Ameer Nausherwan Adil, advocate for respondent It is contended that the impugned order is merely four pages of reproduction leading to one paragraph worth of dissonant conclusion. It was argued that the impugned order has been rendered in a perfunctory manner, not befitting the last fact-finding forum in the statutory hierarchy. Today it is jointly submitted that the impugned order may be set aside and the matter remanded back to the learned Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
